l	Z	d
Command line tool to bisect failing CPython tests.
Find the test_os test method which alters the environment:
    ./python -m test.bisect_cmd --fail-env-changed test_os
Find a reference leak in "test_os", write the list of failing tests into the
"bisect" file:
    ./python -m test.bisect_cmd -o bisect -R 3:3 test_os
Load an existing list of tests from a file using -i option:
    ./python -m test --list-cases -m FileTests test_os > tests
    ./python -m test.bisect_cmd -i tests test_os
file)
open
print
flush)
filename
tests
name
'/usr/lib64/python3.9/test/bisect_cmd.py
write_tests
Writing %s tests into %s)
lenr
write_output&
join)
argsr
format_shell_args.
executable
extend

_args_from_interpreter_flagsZ"_optim_args_from_interpreter_flags)
cmdr
python_cmd2
testz
--list-casesT)
stdoutZ
universal_newlinesz1Failed to list tests: %s failed with exit code %s)
	test_argsr
PIPE
returncoder
exitr
splitlines)
proc
exitcoder
list_cases9
--matchfilez
+ %s)
tempfileZ
mktempr

	run_testsJ
